_note: Catalogue of the major damaging frost events in the Brazilian arabica belt. Used by scraper.rules.frost_model's backtest/sanity harness to check the severity ladder classifies the historic disasters as 'critical', and by the frontend frost view for historical context. `observed_air_min_c` is a REPRESENTATIVE 2 m air minimum reported for the worst-hit region on the peak night (screen-height; the canopy/grass minimum ran several deg colder). `mechanism` is from the meteorological record of the event. These are well-established figures; precise per-station minima vary. A true blind ERA5 backtest (recomputing the model from raw hourly archive) is a separate step that needs archive-api.open-meteo.com access — see scraper.backfill_frost_backtest.

events.label: Geada Negra (Black Frost)
events.crop_impact: Catastrophic: on the order of 70% of Brazil's coffee trees killed; permanent collapse of Paraná as the leading producing state. Arabica prices roughly tripled through 1976-77. A dry hard freeze under a polar air mass — literally the 'black frost'.
events.source: Widely documented; the benchmark Brazilian frost disaster.

events.label: 1994 double frost
events.crop_impact: Two frosts ~two weeks apart devastated the 1995 crop; the worst since 1975. Arabica prices spiked well over 100%.
events.source: Widely documented; drove the mid-1990s price spike.

events.label: July 2021 frosts
events.crop_impact: Strong polar air mass; clear, calm radiative nights drove canopy minima well below the ~-1.5 °C screen minima. ~10-20% of the 2022/23 crop damaged; arabica futures roughly doubled over the following months.
events.source: Well documented; the most damaging frost in decades.
